task_completion.description: The JStock application window is open and visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application interface is clearly visible in the screenshot.

task_completion.description: The 'Stock Watchlist' tab is the currently active tab in the JStock application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Stock Watchlist' tab is highlighted or brought to the front, showing the watchlist table.

task_completion.description: The agent enters 'TSLA' or 'Tesla' into the stock search or add field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'TSLA' or 'Tesla' is visible in the input box used for adding new stocks to the watchlist.

task_completion.description: Tesla (TSLA) is successfully added and visible in the Stock Watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new row containing 'TSLA' (and potentially 'Tesla Inc.') is visible in the main Stock Watchlist table.

task_completion.description: The previously existing stocks are still present in the watchlist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ticker symbols AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, and NVDA are still visible in the watchlist table alongside TSLA.

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock graphical user interface to add the stock, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal or command prompt windows are open showing scripts or commands modifying JStock data files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual JStock application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes appear within the standard JStock UI components (tables, tabs, search bars) rather than a text editor or a mocked-up image.

integrity.description: The stock was genuinely added to the application's data structure, not just typed over the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: TSLA appears as a properly formatted row in the JStock watchlist table, aligning with the columns of the other stocks.
